title: Spicy Roasted Jamaican Mackerel Clay-Pot
description: A flavorful and spicy Jamaican-inspired mackerel dish made with a clay pot, perfect for meal prep. This recipe is free from nuts and is a great option for a weeknight dinner.

ingredients.name: Jamaican scotch bonnet peppers
ingredients.notes: use gloves when handling peppers for best results

ingredients.name: All-purpose flour
ingredients.notes: dredge mackerel fillets in flour before cooking

instructions: Rinse the mackerel fillets and pat them dry with paper towels.
Season the mackerel fillets with salt and black pepper.
Dredge the mackerel fillets in all-purpose flour, shaking off excess.
Heat the coconut oil in a clay pot over medium-high heat.
Add the onion and garlic to the clay pot and sauté until softened.
Add the scotch bonnet peppers to the clay pot and sauté for an additional minute.
Add the thyme to the clay pot and sauté for 30 seconds.
Add the lime juice to the clay pot and stir to combine.
Add the mackerel fillets to the clay pot and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side.
Transfer the clay pot to the preheated oven and roast for 10-12 minutes, or until the mackerel is cooked through.
Garnish with cilantro and scotch bonnet pepper sauce before serving.
